mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which compress corporate profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal a tightening cycle that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the combined CPI/FEDFUNDS signal falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,900k signals weak demand for construction, which reduces corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST falls below 1900.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 110.5 signals strong manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 110.5.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly rise in GDP (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong economic growth, which increases corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 30% of instances where GDPC1 exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly gain in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signals a tight labor market, which drives corporate wage pressures and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weak aggregate demand, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the combined RSAFS/HOUST signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.
